evidence.text: LTL tons increased 0.9% and LTL shipments increased 0.8% in 2022 compared to 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows slight increases in LTL tons and shipments, indicating growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 18.2% in 2022 compared to 2021, reflecting higher fuel surcharges and a long-term yield management strategy.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant 18.2% increase in LTL revenue per hundredweight reflects strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 19.1% in 2022 compared to 2021, driven by higher LTL revenue per hundredweight and slight tonnage growth.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: 19.1% revenue increase driven by higher pricing and tonnage growth.

evidence.text: LTL shipments increased 0.8% and LTL tons increased 0.9% in 2022 compared to 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Slight increases in LTL shipments and tons suggest volume growth.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly for diesel fuel.

evidence.text: Operating supplies and expenses increased 50.3% in 2022, driven by higher diesel fuel costs, with the average cost per gallon increasing 68.2%.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: 50.3% increase in operating expenses driven by 68.2% higher diesel costs.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, but manageable.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages, and benefits increased 10.1% in 2022, with a 10.4% increase in average full-time employees and annual wage increases.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: 10.1% increase in labor costs with a 10.4% rise in employees.

evidence.text: Operating ratio improved 290 basis points to 70.6% in 2022, and net income margin expanded to 22.0% from 19.7%.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: 290 basis points improvement in operating ratio and expanded net income margin.

evidence.text: Productive labor costs as a percentage of revenue improved to 22.9% in 2022 from 25.1% in 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productive labor costs as a percentage of revenue improved by 2.2 percentage points.

factors.summary: Management is confident with growth initiatives.

evidence.text: The company is investing in workforce, equipment, and technology to support long-term growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence in growth initiatives is supported by investments in workforce, equipment, and technology.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear and well-resourced.

evidence.text: Focus on yield management, operating efficiency, and technology investments to support future growth.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear and well-resourced long-term strategic direction with a focus on yield management, efficiency, and technology.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and labor costs are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Fuel surcharges are indexed to diesel fuel prices, and labor costs are increasing due to wage hikes and employee benefits.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel prices and labor costs are significant risks, with evidence of increasing labor costs.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode.

evidence.text: Average number of active full-time employees increased 10.4% in 2022, with hiring to support shipment trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth mode indicated by a 10.4% increase in full-time employees to support shipment trends.
